titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Yale Editions of the Private Papers of James Boswell Project Records
Yale Editions of the Private Papers of James Boswell Project Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig, Jeanne Lowrey, Sandra Markham, Rebecca Hastings

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday May 13, 2026 at 03:01

did.unittitle: Yale Editions of the Private Papers of James Boswell Project records
did.abstract: The records consist of records documenting the activity of the Yale Editions of the Private Papers of James Boswell office, including office files, correspondence, and workproduct generated by the project. The collection also includes scrapbooks and photostats of correspondence and clippings documenting the purchase of James Boswell's private papers by Yale University.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 32-E (YRG 32-E), Records of Yale University Library programs, projects, and committees.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to the records is partially restricted. See Collection Contents for details.
Some records in this finding aid have been redacted, as they include personnel files and other restricted data. These records will not appear in the published finding aid.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult the Request Digitization information on the using special collections website.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: 8 folders containing original letters and cables to, and copies of letters and cables from, Lt. Col. Ralph H. Isham, being his correspondence with Joyce Lady Talbot and others concerning the purchase of the Boswell papers were returned to donor, June 2008.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Yale Editions of the Private Papers of James Boswell Project Records (RU 469). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged by accession.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist of scrapbooks and photostats of correspondence and clippings documenting the purchase of James Boswell's private papers by Yale University. Records also include administrative records such as correspondence, research materials, published volumes, and audiovisual material created in the course of the work of the Yale Editions of the Private Papers of James Boswell office

bioghist.head: Historical Note
bioghist.p: The Yale Editions of the Private Papers of James Boswell was established at Yale University in 1949 to publish the papers in two separate editions, a popular series known as the Trade Edition, and a comprehensive scholarly Research Edition comprising the manuscript of the Life of Johnson , the journals, the correspondence, as well as a catalog of the papers at Yale. The project was terminated in 2021, the editorial office's administrative records were transferred to Yale's University Archives in 2022-2024, and the office was closed in 2024.

did.unittitle: Scrapbook and photostats of correspondence and clippings

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

did.unittitle: Scrapbook regarding the purchase of the Boswell papers
27 (18"x24") Photostats of correspondence and clippings regarding the 1949 acquisition of Boswell papers by Yale

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Office files are restricted until January 1, 2053. Personnel files are restricted until January 1, 2094.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult the Request Digitization information on the using special collections website.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Office files are restricted until January 1, 2053.

accessrestrict.p: Personnel files are restricted until January 1, 2094.

did.unittitle: Clippings of reviews and articles documenting the impact of the Boswell project

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings and reviews compiled by the Boswell Project office to document the impact of their work, including reviews relating to specific volumes of work produced by the office. This series also includes material concerning Frank Brady, Frederick Pottle, and Marion Pottle, including photographs.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research. Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult the Request Digitization information on the using special collections website.
